title: Needs Diagnosis
description: Evaluate your strengths, weaknesses and preferences to generate a personalized plan.

questions.question: How much time can you realistically dedicate to English practice daily?
questions.explanation: 30 minutes daily is a great sweet spot. Consistent short sessions beat irregular long ones.

questions.question: Which activity do you enjoy the most?
questions.explanation: Enjoyable activities lead to consistent practice. Choose resources that match your preferences!

questions.question: Which is the BEST resource for improving listening at B2 level?
questions.explanation: TED Talks with subtitles = comprehensible input at B2. Clear speech + visual support + subtitles as scaffolding.

questions.question: A SMART goal must be Specific, Measurable, ___, Relevant, and Time-bound.
questions.explanation: Achievable = the goal must be realistic given your current situation and resources.

questions.question: What is "spaced repetition"?
options: Studying the same thing every day
Reviewing material at increasing intervals based on memory science
questions.explanation: Spaced repetition reviews items at optimal intervals: 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days, etc.

questions.question: Your speaking skill is your weakest area. Which resource should you prioritize?
questions.explanation: To improve speaking, you need to SPEAK. iTalki provides real conversation practice with native speakers.

questions.question: According to Krashen's Input Hypothesis, we learn best when we receive input that is slightly ___ our current level (i+1).
questions.explanation: i+1 = comprehensible input slightly above your level. Too easy = boring. Too hard = frustrating.

questions.question: Which practice schedule is most effective for long-term learning?
options: Random sessions when you feel motivated
questions.explanation: Daily consistent practice builds stronger neural pathways than irregular intensive sessions.

questions.question: How can you measure your speaking progress WITHOUT a teacher?
options: Record yourself monthly and compare with previous recordings
Count the number of words you know
questions.explanation: Self-recording is powerful: you hear your own improvement in pronunciation, fluency, and confidence.

questions.question: The "immersion" strategy means:
options: Surrounding yourself with English: phone, shows, music, thinking
questions.explanation: Immersion = surrounding yourself with the language in daily life. You can create immersion anywhere.

title: Recurso → Habilidad
description: Match each resource with the skill it best develops.

title: Metacognition Vocabulary
description: Learn the words about learning to talk about your own process in English.

cards.back: Technique that reviews material at increasing intervals. It is based on the forgetting curve. Apps like Anki use it.
cards.example: I use spaced repetition to memorize vocabulary — I review words at increasing intervals.

cards.back: We acquire a language when we receive comprehensible input slightly above our level (i+1).
cards.example: Watching English shows with subtitles provides comprehensible input.

cards.back: Surround yourself with the language: telephone in English, series without subtitles, think in English.
cards.example: I created an immersion environment by changing all my devices to English.

cards.back: Cycle of practice → error → correction → improvement. Without feedback, there is no improvement.
cards.example: iTalki provides a great feedback loop — my tutor corrects me in real time.

cards.back: Actively produce the language (speaking, writing), not just receiving it (reading, listening).
cards.example: Writing a journal in English is great output practice.

cards.back: Evaluate your own level and progress. Key to directing your autonomous learning.
cards.example: Regular self-assessment helps me identify areas that need more practice.

cards.back: Goals: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound. Structure for effective objectives.
cards.example: My SMART goal: 3 conversations per week on iTalki for 2 months.

cards.back: Material you can understand mostly (90-95%) with something new. The "sweet spot" to learn.
cards.example: Podcasts for intermediate learners provide comprehensible input at my level.

cards.back: Try to remember information BEFORE seeing it. More effective than simply rereading.
cards.example: I practice active recall by covering the English word and trying to remember it.

cards.back: Person who checks your progress regularly. It keeps you engaged.
cards.example: My accountability partner and I check in every Sunday to review our English goals.

title: Mi Plan SMART
description: Build SMART learning goals in English.

sentences.prompt: Build a SMART goal for speaking practice
sentences.correct: I will have three 30-minute conversations per week on iTalki for two months.

sentences.prompt: Build a sentence about your daily routine
sentences.correct: Every morning I listen to a podcast during my commute.

sentences.prompt: Build a SMART goal for vocabulary
sentences.correct: I will learn 10 new words per week using Anki for three months.

sentences.prompt: Build a SMART goal for reading
sentences.correct: I will read one English article every day for the next month.
I will change my phone and social media to English starting today.

sentences.prompt: Build a sentence about measuring progress
sentences.correct: I will record myself speaking every month to track my progress.

title: Resource Map by Level
description: Explore the best resources organized by skill and level.
